LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF PHILIP HORNE'S MOTION FOR FINDING THAT PRESENT MATTER IS NOT STAYED UNDER BPC 6201 AS TO TRUSTEE, OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO VACATE STAY UNDER BPC 6201(C) TO PROVIDE FOR: 1) RECOVERY AND PRESERVATION OF SETTLEMENT FUND (WASTED BY TRUSTEE), AND 2) PAYMENT OF LIENS NOT SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION (UNDISPUTED LIEN AMOUNTS AND MEDICAL LIENS) IS OFF CALENDAR. CRC 3.1113(D) PROVIDES THAT A MOVING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES SHALL NOT EXCEED 15 PAGES. HERE, EXCLUDING THE FIRST TWO PAGES (NOTICE OF MOTION) AND LAST PAGE (SIGNATURE), MOVANT'S OPENING BRIEF EXCEEDS THE PAGE LIMITATION BY 9 PAGES WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT. THE MOTION IS THEREFORE OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Finding That Present Matter Is Not Stayed Under Bpc 6201 As To Trustee, Or Alternatively, To Vacate Stay Under Bpc 6201(C) To Provide For: 1) Recovery And Preservation Of Settlement Fund (Wasted By Trustee), And 2) Payment Of Liens Not Subject To Arbitration (Undisputed Lien Amounts And Medical Liens)
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Defendant GERALDINE ARMENDARIZ, Esq. (Tel: 415-986-0873) represeting herself;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PHILIP HORNE's Motion For Finding That Present Matter Is Not Stayed Under Bpc 6201 As To Trustee, Or Alternatively, To Vacate Stay Under BPC 6201(C) To Provide For: 1) Recovery And Preservation Of Settlement Fund (Wasted By Trustee), And 2) Payment Of Liens Not Subject To Arbitration (Undisputed Lien Amounts And Medical Liens).

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to find that this case is not stayed, or alternatively to lift the stay is denied.

First, Business and Professions Code sec. 6201(b) stays this entire action. Even though plaintiff's claims against attorney Armendariz are not subject to arbitration, plaintiff's claims against Soto are stayed, and Soto may stay the entire "action." Business & Professions Code sec. 6201(b) ["If an attorney commences an action in any court or any other proceeding and the client is entitled to maintain arbitration under this article, the client may stay the action or other proceeding by serving and filing a request for arbitration." (emphasis added)]. Second, even if plaintiff's claims against Armendariz are not automatically stayed, there is good cause to do so. The arbitrator's resolution of the fee issue may resolve this case and the court's inherent powers support a stay. CCP 128(a)(8) [court's inherent powers to "control its process"]. Third, there is no reason to lift the stay. Plaintiff contends that his entitlement to $176,000 is undisputed and he apparently seeks to lift the stay to recover that sum. But Soto does dispute the amount of plaintiff's fee. Soto Opposition, 2:27 ["Mr. Soto denies that there are undisputed lien amounts except for the medical liens"]. Lifting the stay to allow plaintiff to prosecute his claims against Armendariz while continuing to stay plaintiff's claims against Soto will lead to duplicative or unnecessary judicial work.

The parties are encouraged to agree to the deposit of the funds into the Armendariz trust account since they all apparently support such relief. Armendariz Opposition, 3:22-27; Soto Opposition, 2:22-23; Horne Moving Brief, 8:21-9:1.

Order signed.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/CK)

MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF PHILIP HORNE Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion For Finding That Present Matter Is Not Stayed Under Bpc 6201 As To Trustee, Or Alternatively, To Vacate Stay Under Bpc 6201(C) To Provide For: 1) Recovery And Preservation Of Settlement Fund (Wasted By Trustee), And 2) Payment Of Liens Not Subject To Arbitration (Undisputed Lien Amounts And Medical Liens)

No appearance.

The tentative ruling was adopted.

PLAINTIFF PHILIP HORNE's Motion For Finding That Present Matter Is Not Stayed Under Bpc 6201 As To Trustee, Or Alternatively, To Vacate Stay Under Bpc 6201(C) To Provide For: 1) Recovery And Preservation Of Settlement Fund (Wasted By Trustee), And 2) Payment Of Liens Not Subject To Arbitration (Undisputed Lien Amounts And Medical Liens) is

OFF CALENDAR. CRC 3.1113(d) provides that a moving points and authorities shall not exceed 15 pages. Here, excluding the first two pages (notice of motion) and last page (signature), movant's opening brief exceeds the page limitation by 9 pages without leave of court. The motion is therefore off calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
